name: Q2581484
abstract: Willie Henry Ruff Jr. (September 1, 1931 – December 24, 2023) was an American jazz musician, specializing in the French horn and double bass, and a music scholar and educator, primarily as a Yale professor from 1971 to 2017.

concerts.label: Robert Helps, Piano, Isidore Cohen, Violin, and Willie Ruff, Horn

works.t: Adagio and Allegro in A-flat Major, Op. 70
Postlude for Horn, Violin and Piano

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Mitchell-Ruff Trio
Yale in New York: Voices of American Music

works.t: Lew Leslie's Blackbirds of 1930: Memories of You

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: the university eventually started to bring Mitchell and Ruff there... I had recordings of Dwight Mitchell and Willie Ruff... once they heard Mitchell and Ruff, ah, they were the darlings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Garland
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer introduces Willie Ruff and his work, and the interviewee responds with 'Yeah.' without adding personal details or confirming a direct relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: On the panel were people like David Baker, and Dan Morgenstern. Then we had Father Norman O’Connor, we had Willie Ruff at a time, and there was Mill Hinton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: I then found out about John Clark and then I found about David Amram. Hopefully you could do a thousand interviews with them. Willie Ruff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: my trio, and Willie Ruff and Dwike Mitchell. Apparently it was a big thing that a black and white group could work under the same roof.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten letters from the Dean, and Willie Ruff, who is head of the Jazz Department there at Yale.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
